exception_detail.claim: The tool owns the distinct transition from a terminal failed writer to one operator-bound replacement writer while preserving the original task and receipt as immutable evidence.
exception_detail.evidence: Exact workspace, branch, head, clean-state, scope, lifecycle and lease gates; one-attempt limit; append-only writer history; hash-bound task-start reconciliation; attempt-specific receipt collection; focused negative and end-to-end collection tests.

grabowski_agent_workspace_writer_handoff.operation_alternative_considered: A generic terminal, task-start or tmux operation was rejected because none can atomically preserve original writer evidence, enforce the one-handoff state transition, reconcile an ambiguous start by exact task identity and bind collection to the replacement receipt.
grabowski_agent_workspace_writer_handoff.rationale: Writer handoff changes the authoritative Agent Workspace state machine rather than merely running a command. It must own a narrowly typed transition that cannot be represented by the broader terminal or durable-task surfaces without bypassing workspace evidence and lease invariants.

exception_detail.claim: The tool owns a distinct browser-managed local form authority with no general JavaScript, field-content disclosure or arbitrary-navigation surface.
exception_detail.evidence: Exact origin and remote-IP binding, owner-held port lease, pre-action audit intent, action-scope confirmation, fixed CDP methods, cleanup fallbacks, redacted receipts and focused negative tests.

grabowski_browser_worker_stored_form_act.operation_alternative_considered: A generic browser or terminal operation was rejected because it would permit caller-controlled script execution or expose browser-managed field contents. This tool owns only the fixed CDP sequence required for one local-device form action and returns receipt-safe booleans and hashes.
grabowski_browser_worker_stored_form_act.rationale: Expose one exact, target-bound browser-managed form action instead of arbitrary caller-supplied CDP or JavaScript authority; bind the action to a running worker, its owner-held loopback port, a canonical local origin, the browser-observed remote address, hashed selectors and an explicit call-scope confirmation.

tool_contract.reversibility: target-dependent; fields cleared on failed or unobserved submission

exception_detail.claim: grabowski_bureau_candidate_assess owns only its declared Bureau operator-intake boundary.
exception_detail.evidence: Digest-bound private artifacts, runtime package identity checks, typed Bureau payload passthrough, exact short leases, no merge path and focused success/conflict/ambiguity tests.

grabowski_bureau_candidate_assess.operation_alternative_considered: A generic operation or terminal command was rejected because it would expose broader authority and would not preserve Bureau domain errors, exact resource bindings, adapter idempotency and ambiguity handling.
grabowski_bureau_candidate_assess.rationale: Canonical candidate assessment is a bounded Bureau read contract; a generic command would widen authority and obscure advisory-only semantics.

exception_detail.claim: grabowski_bureau_candidate_record owns only its declared Bureau operator-intake boundary.
exception_detail.evidence: Digest-bound private artifacts, runtime package identity checks, typed Bureau payload passthrough, exact short leases, no merge path and focused success/conflict/ambiguity tests.

grabowski_bureau_candidate_record.operation_alternative_considered: A generic operation or terminal command was rejected because it would expose broader authority and would not preserve Bureau domain errors, exact resource bindings, adapter idempotency and ambiguity handling.
grabowski_bureau_candidate_record.rationale: Canonical source-bound candidate intake is a distinct Bureau authority; a generic terminal call would expose arbitrary command execution and lose typed idempotency and provenance.

exception_detail.claim: grabowski_bureau_task_propose owns only its declared Bureau operator-intake boundary.
exception_detail.evidence: Digest-bound private artifacts, runtime package identity checks, typed Bureau payload passthrough, exact short leases, no merge path and focused success/conflict/ambiguity tests.

grabowski_bureau_task_propose.operation_alternative_considered: A generic operation or terminal command was rejected because it would expose broader authority and would not preserve Bureau domain errors, exact resource bindings, adapter idempotency and ambiguity handling.
grabowski_bureau_task_propose.rationale: Proposal creation owns immutable digest-bound adapter artifacts while leaving Registry and Queue untouched; generic filesystem and terminal surfaces are broader.

exception_detail.claim: grabowski_bureau_task_publish owns only its declared Bureau operator-intake boundary.
exception_detail.evidence: Digest-bound private artifacts, runtime package identity checks, typed Bureau payload passthrough, exact short leases, no merge path and focused success/conflict/ambiguity tests.

grabowski_bureau_task_publish.operation_alternative_considered: A generic operation or terminal command was rejected because it would expose broader authority and would not preserve Bureau domain errors, exact resource bindings, adapter idempotency and ambiguity handling.
grabowski_bureau_task_publish.rationale: Publication combines Bureau-owned validation with exact short leases, bounded receipt readback and no merge authority; splitting across generic tools would weaken the atomic contract.

exception_detail.claim: grabowski_bureau_task_publish_preview owns only its declared Bureau operator-intake boundary.
exception_detail.evidence: Digest-bound private artifacts, runtime package identity checks, typed Bureau payload passthrough, exact short leases, no merge path and focused success/conflict/ambiguity tests.

grabowski_bureau_task_publish_preview.operation_alternative_considered: A generic operation or terminal command was rejected because it would expose broader authority and would not preserve Bureau domain errors, exact resource bindings, adapter idempotency and ambiguity handling.
grabowski_bureau_task_publish_preview.rationale: Publication preview exposes exact Bureau validation and lease requirements without effect; it is narrower than generic command execution.

exception_detail.claim: grabowski_bureau_task_review owns only the exact hash-bound Bureau proposal-review authority boundary.
exception_detail.evidence: Caller supplies proposal id, reviewer and exact proposal digest; no caller-controlled review timestamp exists; Bureau remains the source of review semantics and timestamp; Registry, Queue and publication truth are not mutated; focused input, symlink and call-shape tests cover the adapter boundary.

grabowski_bureau_task_review.operation_alternative_considered: A generic terminal or operation call was rejected because it would expose broader command authority and would not preserve typed proposal identity, exact digest binding, Bureau domain errors, ambiguity readback and machine discoverability of the review transition.
grabowski_bureau_task_review.rationale: Proposal review is the distinct approval transition between immutable proposal creation and publication preview. A narrow typed tool exposes exactly that boundary while delegating timestamp and domain truth to Bureau.

exception_detail.claim: This tool owns only validation and bounded projection of the canonical coding-agent inventory.
exception_detail.evidence: Read-only annotation, empty effects, bounded regular-file reads, schema validation and focused regression tests.

grabowski_coding_agent_catalog.operation_alternative_considered: A generic operation or a mode flag on the route tool was rejected because it would create a union-shaped interface and hide the prerequisite inventory and quota truth behind task-specific arguments.
grabowski_coding_agent_catalog.rationale: The normalized model, harness, route and quota graph is a bounded inventory read with no task-decision inputs and must remain directly discoverable before requesting a recommendation.

exception_detail.claim: This tool owns only deterministic advisory model-route selection and grants no dispatch, mutation, merge or deployment authority.
exception_detail.evidence: Read-only annotation, empty effects, explicit nonclaims, fail-closed cost gates, provider-independent review tests and automatic_execution_authorized=false.

grabowski_coding_agent_route.operation_alternative_considered: Extending grabowski_agent_execution_route was rejected because that tool selects workspace and competition shape, while this tool ranks exact zero-cost model routes against live quota evidence; merging them would couple independent inputs and produce a broad unstable union response.
grabowski_coding_agent_route.rationale: Exact model, harness and quota selection is a materially different decision layer from the existing R0-R3 workspace-shape recommendation and requires typed task-size, novelty, risk and review inputs.

exception_detail.claim: grabowski_juno_pair owns only its declared Juno device authority.
exception_detail.evidence: Bounded protocol handling, exact identity checks, private key handling, receipts and integration tests.

grabowski_juno_pair.operation_alternative_considered: A generic operation was rejected because the private device protocol, local consent, exact instance binding and receipts require one cohesive typed boundary.
grabowski_juno_pair.rationale: Pair one exact device session only after short-lived local consent and session escalation.

exception_detail.claim: grabowski_juno_run owns only its declared Juno device authority.
exception_detail.evidence: Bounded protocol handling, exact identity checks, private key handling, receipts and integration tests.

grabowski_juno_run.operation_alternative_considered: A generic operation was rejected because the private device protocol, local consent, exact instance binding and receipts require one cohesive typed boundary.
grabowski_juno_run.rationale: Run one bounded, digest-bound device job through the paired Juno worker.

exception_detail.claim: grabowski_juno_status owns only its declared Juno device authority.
exception_detail.evidence: Bounded protocol handling, exact identity checks, private key handling, receipts and integration tests.

grabowski_juno_status.operation_alternative_considered: A generic operation was rejected because the private device protocol, local consent, exact instance binding and receipts require one cohesive typed boundary.
grabowski_juno_status.rationale: Provide bounded observation of the private Juno device worker without generic command access.

exception_detail.claim: The tool exclusively owns exact in-band blockade disarm.
exception_detail.evidence: Hash-bound preconditions, external-stop denial, recovery checks, quarantine receipts and restore-on-audit-failure tests.

grabowski_operator_blockade_disarm.operation_alternative_considered: A typed operation was rejected because the generic operation authority cannot safely bypass a blockade while preserving exact marker identity, rollback and recovery-gate requirements.
grabowski_operator_blockade_disarm.rationale: Disarm must remain a narrowly evidence-bound recovery authority distinct from ordinary mutation and require exact hashes, fresh recovery evidence and reversible quarantine.

exception_detail.claim: The tool exclusively owns canonical typed blockade engagement.
exception_detail.evidence: Generic filesystem tools and explicit direct-command targets reject the marker; active strong scoped blockades also close opaque command channels. Same-UID out-of-band execution is not claimed as a kernel boundary.

grabowski_operator_blockade_engage.operation_alternative_considered: A typed operation was rejected because generic operation execution would inherit broader terminal or file authority and could not enforce the exact marker transaction contract.
grabowski_operator_blockade_engage.rationale: The typed operator surface exposes one create-only, schema-valid, audit-bound authority for canonical blockade engagement; generic filesystem tools and explicitly targeted direct-command arguments are denied.

exception_detail.claim: The tool exclusively owns exact typed legacy-to-root blockade migration.
exception_detail.evidence: Exact ID and hash preconditions, migration-intent and completion audits, create-only root publication, operator-local exact legacy removal, dual-marker retention on cleanup failure and post-state readback tests.

grabowski_operator_blockade_migrate_lega.operation_alternative_considered: A generic operation was rejected because ordinary mutation is correctly blocked by the legacy marker and cannot obtain the root-owned parent, peer-cgroup, create-first and exact rollback invariants.
grabowski_operator_blockade_migrate_lega.rationale: Legacy migration must remain a dedicated create-first authority that can operate while the legacy hard stop is active, bind exact preimage hashes, publish the root-owned marker before locally removing the unchanged legacy record and retain both markers fail-closed if cleanup cannot be proven.

exception_detail.claim: The tool owns only immutable blockade observation authority.
exception_detail.evidence: Read-only annotation, empty effects, scoped decision tests and no filesystem mutation.

grabowski_operator_blockade_status.operation_alternative_considered: A typed operation was rejected because operation dispatch itself is mutation-gated and cannot guarantee the immutable status lane during a blockade.
grabowski_operator_blockade_status.rationale: Blockade observation must remain available while ordinary mutation authority is frozen and therefore requires a separately reviewable immutable recovery surface.

exception_detail.claim: This tool adds only bounded read authority over immutable task archive catalog metadata and does not expand mutation or deletion authority.
exception_detail.evidence: The implementation exposes only manifest-verified segment summaries, uses file_read capability, and regression tests prove no mutation effect.

grabowski_task_archive_list.operation_alternative_considered: A generic operation was rejected because archive discovery is a recurring read-only consumer surface whose cursor and integrity state must remain typed and directly inspectable.
grabowski_task_archive_list.rationale: Archive discovery requires a stable bounded paginated catalog shape with explicit manifest-only integrity semantics distinct from reading record payloads.

exception_detail.claim: This tool adds only bounded read authority over fully verified immutable task archive segments and does not expand mutation or deletion authority.
exception_detail.evidence: The implementation verifies the entire bounded segment before returning records, uses file_read capability, and exposes explicit non-claims.

grabowski_task_archive_read.operation_alternative_considered: A generic operation was rejected because segment-bound full verification and view-bound pagination need a stable typed read contract separate from catalog listing.
grabowski_task_archive_read.rationale: Archive record drill-down requires a segment identifier, full integrity verification, and record pagination that are materially different from catalog discovery.

ipad_capability_manifest.operation_alternative_considered: A generic operation was rejected because local iPadOS consent, security-scoped bookmark resolution, exact agent-instance binding and device receipts form one private device boundary.
ipad_capability_manifest.rationale: Expose one exact locally consented iPad storage operation instead of arbitrary device code; bind it to agent instance, grant evidence, provider, path and operation-specific preconditions.

repoground_bundle_discover.rationale: The canonical RepoGround identity must be directly selectable as the sole supported public interface after the explicit hard cut.

exception_detail.claim: This tool adds one bounded read-only composition call shape without expanding mutation authority.
exception_detail.evidence: Tests bind base and target commits plus the Git tree delta, enforce a hard context budget, separate dirty overlay state, and expose used and skipped retrieval lanes.

repoground_context_compose.operation_alternative_considered: Client-side composition over context_pack, impact, symbol, call-graph, citation, entry and delta reads was rejected because it would duplicate budget and diff-binding policy across agents and could produce unbounded or inconsistent context.
repoground_context_compose.rationale: Change-oriented agents need one deterministic, diff-bound and budgeted composition surface; the general context pack has materially different inputs and does not bind base/target revisions or report retrieval-lane decisions.

repoground_find_symbol.operation_alternative_considered: A generic query operation was rejected because it would collapse symbol identity, graph direction, and S0/S1 negative semantics into an untyped result.
repoground_find_symbol.rationale: Exact symbol-index lookup is a distinct bounded read shape that cannot be represented faithfully by free-text retrieval without losing symbol identity.

operations.description: Inspect one user service without changing its state.
Run a bounded hostname probe on one registered host.
Restart one user service and verify that it becomes active.
